playlist_title: Requiem Concert in Remembrance of President John F. Kennedy

videos.title: Opening Remarks by Matthew Ogden: Msgr. Murray & President Higgins
Helga Zepp-LaRouche, Founder and President of the Schiller Institute
videos.description: Opening Remarks by Helga Zepp-LaRouche, Founder and President of the Schiller Institute, to Schiller Institute Concert of W.A. Mozart's Requiem in D minor, A Remembrance of President John F. Kennedy on the 50th Anniversary of His Death & A Recommitment to the Principles of His Presidency.

videos.title: Opening Remarks: Former Congressmen Gallagher
videos.description: Greetings from former U.S. Congressman Cornelius Gallagher to Schiller Institute Concert of W.A. Mozart's Requiem in D minor, A Remembrance of President John F. Kennedy on the 50th Anniversary of His Death & A Recommitment to the Principles of His Presidency

videos.title: A Remembrance of President John F. Kennedy: W.A. Mozart - Requiem

chapters.title: August 17, 1962: Remarks in Pueblo, Colo. following approval of the Fryingpan Arkansas Project.
August 18, 1962: Remarks in Los Banos, Calif., all the groundbreaking ceremonies for the San Luis Dam
October 3, 1963: Remarks in Heber Springs, Ark.. at the dedication of Greers Ferry Dam.
September 12. 1962: Address at Rice University in Houston on the nation's space effort.
September 26, 1963: Remarks at the Hanford, Wash., Electric Generating Plant
October 22, 1963: Address at the Anniversary Convocation of the National Academy of Sciences, Washington D.C.
October 26, 1963: Remarks at the groundbreaking for the Robert Frost Library at Amherst College, Amherst, Mass

videos.description:
Schiller Institute Chorus and Friends perform W.A. Mozart's Requiem in D minor, as A Remembrance of President John F. Kennedy on the 50th Anniversary of His Death & A Recommitment to the Principles of His Presidency. 
Performed at the scientific Verdi Tuning of C = 256 Hz.

For the text of the clips from selection of addresses by President John F. Kennedy, 
as the Interlude in the performance of Mozart's Requiem, visit the concert page at http://bit.ly/1b6tNsF
